supervisor.reason: Daemonize now reuses dormammu run --prompt-file semantics, examples/docs are aligned, and regression validation passed.

bootstrap.goal: Simplify daemonize so queued prompts reuse dormammu run --prompt-file semantics instead of phase-specific daemon CLI config.

tasks.resume_checkpoint: Implementation and validation are complete. Resume from commit preparation only if a follow-up asks for staging or committing this daemonize simplification.

items.text: Phase 1. Simplify daemonize config so it no longer accepts phase-specific coding agent CLI settings
Phase 2. Rework daemonize to process each queued prompt through the same supervised loop behavior as `dormammu run --prompt-file <path>`
Phase 3. Update daemon examples, docs, and regression coverage for the simplified config and run-loop reuse

next_action: Await direction on whether to prepare a commit for the validated daemonize simplification.
notes: Treat this file as machine truth and keep Markdown synchronized.
DASHBOARD.md should show the actual progress of the active scope.
PLAN.md should list prompt-derived development items in `[ ] Phase N. <title>` format.
The installed guidance bundle and packaged asset copy should stay synchronized.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
A new prompt-driven scope now targets daemonized prompt watching from JSON configuration.
The repository does not currently contain .dev/PROJECT.md or .dev/ROADMAP.md, so planning is grounded in the direct request, AGENTS.md, current code, and machine state.
Daemon prompt execution remains serialized so only one prompt file advances at a time and later files remain pending until the active prompt is completed.
This scope removes the custom daemon phase pipeline in favor of reusing the existing supervised run loop.
Daemon config examples and docs must move with the schema change so operators no longer configure phase-specific agent CLIs in daemonize.json.
Validation completed successfully for tests.test_daemon, tests.test_cli, and tests.test_loop_runner.
